objections.description: The roadmap presents a disorganized feature laundry list rather than a workflow-centric execution plan. It lists six parallel workstreams without sequencing for research utility, forcing engineering to guess at dependencies.
objections.suggested_fix: Explicitly sequence three phases: (1) Persistent App Shell with Research Session history/queue, (2) Assisted Search with DOU-specific facets, (3) Virtualized Document Reader with deep-linking. Defer all polish items.

objections.description: Assisted search is hand-wavy vaporware. No specification of the assistance mechanism (autocomplete? boolean syntax? entity extraction?) or DOU-specific affordances like date-range filtering or section facets, which are critical for gazette research.
objections.suggested_fix: Define assisted search as: faceted query builder with date range picker, DOU section filters (Admin, Judiciário, etc.), and persistent query history with result highlighting.

objections.description: Claims to support very long DOU documents but gaps analysis omits virtualization or incremental loading strategies. Rendering a 10,000-item Diário Oficial in a browser without virtualization will crash mobile and lag desktop.
objections.suggested_fix: Mandate virtualized scrolling (react-window or similar) and section-based lazy loading as non-negotiable prerequisites to TOC and reading position features.

objections.description: Missing multi-document comparison workflow. DOU research is fundamentally cross-referential (comparing editions, checking retificações), yet the roadmap only supports single-document reading.
objections.suggested_fix: Add Phase 2 requirement for tabbed document interface or split-view comparison mode; if out of scope, explicitly document this limitation.

suggestions.description: PRIORITY 1: Operational Dashboard App Shell. Replace hero with dense research cockpit: recent documents queue, saved search alerts, and reading progress summary. This maximizes researcher efficiency immediately.
PRIORITY 2: Assisted Search with Context. Implement faceted filters (date, section, act type) and result snippets with hit-highlighting. DOU documents are too long for blind navigation.
PRIORITY 3: High-Performance Document Reader. Virtualized rendering + TOC sidebar + URL hash deep-linking + localStorage scroll position restoration. Enables reliable citation and resumption.

DEFERRED: Motion tokens (decorative), PDF/share-at-position (browsers handle this), concurso parser (narrow use case), and mobile-first emphasis (DOU research is desktop-primary; mobile is secondary).
